annotations.label: Abacus (structure)

anchor.snippet: consists of a two-dimensional array of slidable beads

annotations.note: A physical computing device, not a mathematical object formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rod as positional digit

anchor.snippet: Each rod typically represents one digit of a multi-digit number

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Nat.digits b n` formalizes positional digit representation, the math underlying one-rod-per-digit, but not the abacus itself.

annotations.label: Bi-quinary coded decimal layout

anchor.snippet: use a system resembling bi-quinary coded decimal

annotations.note: Bi-quinary coded decimal is a specific physical encoding scheme not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Supported arithmetic operations

anchor.snippet: supports multiple methods to perform calculations

annotations.note: A vague claim about device capabilities, not a precise statement to formalize.

annotations.label: Sumerian sexagesimal columns

anchor.snippet: delimited the successive orders of magnitude of their sexagesimal

annotations.note: Base-60 place value is captured by `Nat.digits 60`, but the historical Sumerian column system is not formalized.

annotations.label: Roman abacus groove values

anchor.snippet: denote fives (five units, five tens, etc.) resembling a bi-quinary coded decimal system

annotations.note: A description of physical groove values on the Roman abacus, not a mathematical theorem.

annotations.label: Suanpan bead system

anchor.snippet: to represent numbers in a bi-quinary coded decimal -like system

annotations.note: The suanpan bead configuration is a physical encoding, not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Top and bottom bead values

anchor.snippet: One of the top beads is 5, while one of the bottom beads is 1

annotations.note: Assignment of bead values is a property of the physical device, not a Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: Hexadecimal capability

anchor.snippet: allows use with a hexadecimal numeral system (or any base up to 18)

annotations.note: Arbitrary-base representation is formalized via `Nat.digits b` for any base, though the abacus's bead-limited base range is not.

annotations.label: School bead frame unit value

anchor.snippet: each bead represents one unit irrespective of which wire it is on

annotations.note: A description of unit-valued beads on a teaching frame, not a mathematical object.

annotations.label: Representing 42

anchor.snippet: 42 can be represented by shifting all 10 beads on four wires and two beads on a fifth wire

annotations.note: A concrete bead-shifting illustration on a physical frame, not a formalized statement.

annotations.label: Multiplying 6 times 7

anchor.snippet: may be represented by shifting 7 beads on 6 wires, subsequently rearranged to show 42

annotations.note: An informal demonstration of 6×7=42 on beads; not a named declaration in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Binary abacus switch

anchor.snippet: each bead represents a switch which can be either

annotations.note: Binary representation is captured by `Nat.digits 2`, but the bead-as-switch device is not formalized.

annotations.label: Nepohualtzintzin base-20 system

anchor.snippet: used a 5-digit base-20 system

annotations.note: Base-20 positional representation is expressible via `Nat.digits 20`, though the Nepohualtzintzin device is not formalized.

annotations.label: Bead values per row

anchor.snippet: Beads in the first row have unitary values (1, 2, 3, and 4)

annotations.note: A description of per-row bead values on a specific device, not a Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: Upper-row multiply-by-20 rule

anchor.snippet: it is enough to multiply by 20 (by each row), the value of the corresponding count in the first row

annotations.note: The place-value scaling by the base per row is the structure of `Nat.ofDigits b`, but this device-specific rule is not formalized.

annotations.label: 13 rows of 7 beads

anchor.snippet: The device featured 13 rows with 7 beads, 91 in total

annotations.note: A factual count (13×7=91) about a device, not a named theorem in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nepohualtzintzin day counts

anchor.snippet: represented the number of days that a season of the year lasts

annotations.note: A calendrical/historical interpretation, not a mathematical statement formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Yupana Fibonacci place values

anchor.snippet: calculations were based using the Fibonacci sequence 1, 1, 2, 3, 5 and powers of 10, 20, and 40 as place values

annotations.note: The Fibonacci sequence is formalized as `Nat.fib`, but the historical yupana place-value scheme itself is not.

annotations.label: Abacus-based mental calculation

anchor.snippet: is the act of performing calculations, including addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division, in the mind by manipulating an imagined abacus

annotations.note: A cognitive technique, not a mathematical object formalized in Mathlib.
